start: Space sweeper, clocking in. The cosmos is filthy again and apparently I'm the only one with a broom.
wave_1: Swept. Not that anyone will send a card.
wave_2: Planet saved. Again. Try to contain the parades.
wave_3: All that debris, gone. Rather like my enthusiasm.
wave_4: Another sector spotless. I'm ecstatic. This is me, ecstatic.
wave_5: An entire formation, filed under swept. They'll only send another.
wave_6: Filed the rubble under D, for dealt with.
wave_7: The empire keeps printing invaders. I keep un-printing them. Nobody says thank you.
saucer_big: Oh good, an imperial cruiser. They never wipe their boots.
Another cruiser. I do hope they brought a mop this time. They never do.
Ah, the empire. Sending capital ships to fight a man with a broom.
A cruiser. Do they teach parking at the academy? Clearly not.
Here comes several billion credits of hardware, aimed at me. Lovely.
The empire's finest. Which, frankly, explains quite a lot.
A cruiser drops by. I shall add 'existential dread' to today's chores.
Big, shiny, over-armed, and lost. Typical imperial tourist.
That's a warship. I own a broom. This seems unbalanced.
Another cruiser. I've stopped waving. It never waves back.
saucer_down: Imperial litter, swept. His majesty can invoice me.
death_1: Ow. That was my favourite hull.
power_up: Ooh, a shiny thing. I suppose I'll take it. It won't help my mood.
one_up: Another life. Another shift, more like.
game_over: Planet doomed. I'd say I'm surprised, but my surprise circuits were never fitted.
hiscore: A new record. The universe remains, as ever, unimpressed.
warp_in: Something's warping in. It's never a tea trolley.
close_call: They're getting rather close. I'd complain to management, if I had any.
invasion: Rows and rows of them, marching in step. So organised. So sweepable.
